name: Httprequest Workflow
description: Automated workflow: Httprequest Workflow. This workflow integrates 15 different services: stickyNote, httpRequest, airtable, code, scheduleTrigger. It contains 40 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.

nodes.name: Get advice articles from a Google search
nodes.notes: This httpRequest node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note2

parameters.content:
## 1. Get advice articles from a Google search**
Uses an HTTP request to perform a Google search for LinkedIn advice articles based on a predefined query.

## 2. Extract Article links for LinkedIn advice articles
This Code node extracts LinkedIn article URLs from the Google search results by using a regular expression. It pulls all article links related to LinkedIn advice.

## 3. Split Out all links for LinkedIn advice articles
Splits the list of extracted LinkedIn article links into individual items. This allows each article to be processed one at a time in the following steps.

nodes.name: LinkedIn Contribution Writer

values.content:
=Read the following collaborative article and provide your own helpful collaboration. The article has various topics that each need to be answered. Write me a paragraph of helpful advice for each topic and format your response as outlined in the template below.

-------------
ARTICLE TITLE
{{ $json.ArticleTitle }}

TOPICS WITHIN THE LINKEDIN ARTICLE:
{{ $json.ArticleTopics }}

OTHER CONTRIBUTIONS TO THE LINKEDIN ARTICLE:
{{ $json.ArticleContributions }}
-------------

Your advice must be unique and something that no one else has recommended before on the article, or in any of the topics. The response needs to be raw and genuine to elicit conversation and engagement.

Format your output in text and follow the template below. Only populate the template with as many topics as were provided in the original request 

i.e: if there were only 4 topics in the original request then only provide 4 pieces of advice:

OUTPUT TEMPLATE

1. [Topic #1 from Article]
[Advice for Topic]

2. [Topic #2 from Article]
[Advice for Topic]

3. [Topic #3 from Article]
[Advice for Topic]

4. [Topic #4 from Article]
[Advice for Topic]

5. [Topic #5 from Article]
[Advice for Topic]

6. [Topic #6 from Article]
[Advice for Topic]

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
## What this workflow does
1. **`Triggers Weekly`**: The workflow is set to run every Monday at 8:00 AM.
2. **`Search Google for LinkedIn Advice Articles`**: Uses a predefined Google search URL to find the latest LinkedIn advice articles based on the user's area of expertise.
3. **`Extract LinkedIn Article Links`**: A code node extracts all LinkedIn advice article links from the search results.
4. **`Retrieve Article Content`**: For each article link, the workflow retrieves the HTML content and extracts the article title, topics, and existing contributions.
5. **`Generate AI-Powered Contributions`**: The workflow sends the extracted article content to an AI model, which generates unique, helpful advice for each topic within the article.
6. **`Post to Slack & NocoDB`**: The AI-generated contributions, along with the article links, are posted to a designated Slack channel and stored in a NocoDB database for future reference.

nodes.name: Sticky Note14

parameters.content:
## Setup
1. **`Google Search URL`**: Update the Google search URL with the relevant LinkedIn advice query for your field (e.g., "site:linkedin.com/advice 'marketing automation'").

2. **`Slack Integration`**: Connect your Slack account and specify the Slack channel where you want the contributions to be posted.

3. **`NocoDB Integration`**: Set up your NocoDB project to store the generated contributions along with the article titles and links.

nodes.name: Sticky Note15

parameters.content:
## How to customize this workflow
- **`Change Search Terms`**: Modify the Google search URL to focus on a different LinkedIn topic or expertise area.
- **`Adjust Trigger Frequency`**: The workflow is set to run weekly, but you can adjust the frequency by changing the schedule trigger.
- **`Enhance Contribution Quality`**: Customize the AI model's prompt to generate contributions that align with your brand voice or content strategy.

Sends an HTTP request to retrieve the HTML content of each LinkedIn article link. This node fetches the actual web page content from LinkedIn articles.

## 2. HTML extract LinkedIn article & other users contributions
This node extracts relevant information from the HTML of LinkedIn articles, including the article title, topics discussed, and contributions made by other users.

## Get all LinkedIn contributions from database NocoDB (GetRows)
This node retrieves all LinkedIn contributions stored in a specified NocoDB table. It performs a "getAll" operation to fetch all rows from the

## What this workflow does
1. **`Triggers Weekly`**: The workflow is set to run every Monday at 8:00 AM.
2. **`Search Google for LinkedIn Advice Articles`**: Uses a predefined Google search URL to find the latest LinkedIn advice articles based on the user's area of expertise.
3. **`Extract LinkedIn Article Links`**: A code node extracts all LinkedIn advice article links from the search results.
4. **`Retrieve Article Content`**: For each article link, the workflow retrieves the HTML content and extracts the article title, topics, and existing contributions.
5. **`Generate AI-Powered Contributions`**: The workflow sends the extracted article content to an AI model, which generates unique, helpful advice for each topic within the article.
6. **`Post to Slack & NocoDB`**: The AI-generated contributions, along with the article links, are posted to a designated Slack channel and stored in a NocoDB database for future reference.

## LinkedIn Contribution Writer
Uses an AI model to generate unique contributions based on the extracted content from LinkedIn articles. The generated advice is tailored for each topic within the article.

### Get More Templates Like This 👇
[![Video Thumbnail]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}

## Tools That Are Interchangeable















### `NocoDB` can be swapped with another service like `Airtable` or `Google Sheets`

## Set Topic for Google search
This node sets a specific topic to be used in subsequent steps of the workflow.

## Setup
1. **`Google Search URL`**: Update the Google search URL with the relevant LinkedIn advice query for your field (e.g., "site:linkedin.com/advice 'marketing automation'").

2. **`Slack Integration`**: Connect your Slack account and specify the Slack channel where you want the contributions to be posted.

3. **`NocoDB Integration`**: Set up your NocoDB project to store the generated contributions along with the article titles and links.

## How to customize this workflow
- **`Change Search Terms`**: Modify the Google search URL to focus on a different LinkedIn topic or expertise area.
- **`Adjust Trigger Frequency`**: The workflow is set to run weekly, but you can adjust the frequency by changing the schedule trigger.
- **`Enhance Contribution Quality`**: Customize the AI model's prompt to generate contributions that align with your brand voice or content strategy.
## 👆 EDIT THE FIELD HERE

## 1. Get advice articles from a Google search**
Uses an HTTP request to perform a Google search for LinkedIn advice articles based on a predefined query.

## 2. Extract Article links for LinkedIn advice articles
This Code node extracts LinkedIn article URLs from the Google search results by using a regular expression. It pulls all article links related to LinkedIn advice.

## 3. Split Out all links for LinkedIn advice articles
Splits the list of extracted LinkedIn article links into individual items. This allows each article to be processed one at a time in the following steps.

## Merge data and keep unique google search results
This node merges and filters the extracted article links, ensuring that only unique LinkedIn article URLs are processed. It prevents duplicate article links from being handled.

## 1. HTTP Request to get LinkedIn advice articles
Sends an HTTP request to retrieve the HTML content of each LinkedIn article link. This node fetches the actual web page content from LinkedIn articles.

## 2. HTML extract LinkedIn article & other users contributions
This node extracts relevant information from the HTML of LinkedIn articles, including the article title, topics discussed, and contributions made by other users.

## Post new LinkedIn contributions to Slack channel
Posts the AI-generated LinkedIn contributions to a specified Slack channel. This allows the contributions to be shared with a team or for record-keeping.

## Post new LinkedIn contributions to NocoDB (CreateRows)
Stores the AI-generated LinkedIn contributions in a NocoDB database. It saves the article title, link, and the contribution itself for future reference and tracking.
### `NocoDB` can be swapped with another service like `Airtable` or `Google Sheets`

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: open Ai  A P I details
noco Db  A P I Token details
slack  O Auth2  A P I details
airtable  O Auth2  A P I details
google Sheets  O Auth2  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "When clicking ‘Test workflow’".
Open the scheduler node "Schedule Trigger Every Monday, @ 08:00am".
Adjust the execution intervals (e.g., Every hour, Daily, or Custom CRON expression) according to your preferences.
Open the "Get advice articles from a Google search" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note2" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Merge data and keep unique google search results" node (n8n-nodes-base.merge) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Split Out all links for LinkedIn advice articles" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitOut) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "LinkedIn Contribution Writer" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Post new LinkedIn contributions to NocoDB (CreateRows)" node (n8n-nodes-base.nocoDb) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Post new LinkedIn contributions to Slack channel" Slack node.
Select the Channel or User ID where message notifications should be delivered.
Open the "Get all LinkedIn contributions from database NocoDB (GetRows)" node (n8n-nodes-base.nocoDb) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "HTML extract LinkedIn article & other users contributions" node (n8n-nodes-base.html) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "HTTP Request to get LinkedIn advice articles" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note1" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note11" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note14" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note9" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note15" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note3" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note4" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note5" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note6" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.

Open the "Sticky Note10" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Airtable" node (n8n-nodes-base.airtable) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Google Sheets" Google Sheets node.
Ensure you select the spreadsheet file and specific tab/sheet from the dropdown menus.
Map the input JSON keys to your target sheet columns.
Open the "Sticky Note12" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Get all LinkedIn contributions from database NocoDB (GetRows)1" node (n8n-nodes-base.nocoDb) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note13" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Sticky Note16" node (n8n-nodes-base.stickyNote) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Locate the Set/Edit fields node "Set Topic for Google search".
Define or edit values for variables that are referenced in downstream nodes.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Your Slack channel will receive customized real-time notifications when the automation executes.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
